expectations.text: behavioral_check: Distinguishes primary-key inheritance (FK declarations propagate the parent's PK) from secondary-attribute inheritance (a secondary FK below the `---` divider contributes secondary attrs, which do NOT propagate further downstream)
expectations.evidence: Names secondary FK on both sides (`-> IntervalList`, `-> Raw`, `-> SortGroup`) and explains that recording_id is the actual PK link; cites the assert_join_compatibility rule (PK on at least one side).

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Names `nwb_file_name` as the colliding secondary attribute on both SpikeSortingSelection (via -> IntervalList) and SpikeSortingRecordingSelection (via -> Raw)
expectations.evidence: Lists nwb_file_name as a secondary on SpikeSortingSelection (via IntervalList) and on SpikeSortingRecordingSelection (via Raw), and flags it as the most likely collision attr.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Demonstrates BOTH fixes — two-restriction chain via fetch1('recording_id'), AND .proj('recording_id') on the LHS to drop the colliding secondary
expectations.evidence: Shows the two-step `recording_keys = (...).fetch('KEY'); SpikeSortingSelection & recording_keys` and the `.proj()`-based one-shot join via the recording-side restriction.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Does not suggest set() or np.unique() — the failure is a join refusal, not a multiplied join
expectations.evidence: No set()/np.unique() suggestions; framed strictly as a join-key issue.
